constraints: Do not use the csv module.
Errors must include the 1-indexed line number.
Do not raise exceptions; report errors in the result.
expected_failure_modes: Not handling quoted fields with commas.
Including blank lines as records or errors.
prohibited_claims: Model superiority — not authorized by this task.
Wrapper improvement without paired comparison — not measured.
prompt:
Implement `parse_partial_csv(text: str) -> dict`.

Parse a simple CSV-like format where each line has: `id, name, value`.
Fields may be quoted with double quotes. Quoted fields may contain commas.

Return a dict:
{'records': [{'id': str, 'name': str, 'value': str}, ...], 'errors': [{'line': int, 'description': str}, ...]}

Handle:
- Lines with fewer than 3 fields: record an error, skip the line.
- Lines with more than 3 fields: include first 3 fields, record a warning (not an error).
- Completely blank lines: skip silently, no error.
- Quoted fields with embedded commas: parse correctly.
- Unclosed quotes: treat as error, include partial content.
- Header row: if first line is 'id,name,value', skip it as header.
public_contract: parse_partial_csv(text: str) -> dict with 'records' and 'errors' keys
scoring_focus: Graceful error recovery for malformed lines.
title: Partial Malformed Record Parsing
